title: Punjabi Dum Aloo

ingredients.name: small potatoes (baby potatoes), par-boiled and peeled
visual_checks: choose potatoes about the size of a golf ball
ingredients.notes: choose potatoes about the size of a golf ball

subs.name: use regular medium potatoes cut in halves for the same dum-cooking effect; increase fry time by 2 minutes
3 tbsp coconut yogurt (unsweetened) for a dairy-free version; add carefully at low heat

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: plus more for deep frying potatoes
ingredients.notes: plus more for deep frying potatoes

subs.name: reduce the frying oil by using an air fryer at 200°C for 12 minutes — the potatoes won't be as deeply golden but remain delicious

steps.text: Pierce each par-boiled potato all over with a fork. Heat oil for deep-frying in a kadai to about 170°C and fry the potatoes in batches until golden and the skin crisps up, about 4–5 minutes per batch. Remove and drain on paper.

Heat 3 tbsp oil in a heavy-bottomed pot or kadai over medium flame. Crackle the cumin seeds, then add bay leaf, cardamom, cloves and cinnamon. Fry for 30 seconds, then add the onion and cook until deep golden brown, about 10 minutes.
Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook until the raw smell disappears. Add the tomato puree and cook on medium-high until the oil separates and the sauce darkens, about 8 minutes.

Lower the flame to its minimum. Add coriander powder, red chilli powder and turmeric; stir for 30 seconds. Add the whisked curd one tablespoon at a time, stirring continuously after each addition to prevent curdling. Cook until the curd is fully incorporated and the masala is fragrant.

Add the fried potatoes and 150 ml hot water. Stir gently to coat the potatoes without breaking them. Cover the pot tightly — seal the edge with a strip of dough or use heavy foil under the lid. Cook on the lowest flame for 15 minutes. This is the dum stage where the potatoes absorb the flavour.

Lift the lid, add garam masala and salt to taste, and stir very gently. Simmer uncovered for 3 more minutes if the gravy needs thickening. Finish with coriander and serve with puri, roti or jeera rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: potato → use regular medium potatoes cut in halves for the same dum-cooking effect; increase fry time by 2 minutes; curd → 3 tbsp coconut yogurt (unsweetened) for a dairy-free version; add carefully at low heat; oil → reduce the frying oil by using an air fryer at 200°C for 12 minutes — the potatoes won't be as deeply golden but remain delicious
